steps.title: Identify the equation components
steps.content: The equation $2x + 35y = 3,934$ has coefficients 2 and 35 representing the park and residential area in hectares. These multiply with variables to sum to 3,934 total trees.

steps.title: Recognize what the equation structure means
steps.content:
For the equation to equal the total number of trees, each product (area × variable) must represent the trees in that region. Therefore:
$$2x = \text{trees in park}$$
$$35y = \text{trees in residential area}$$

steps.title: Interpret x
steps.content:
Since $2x$ equals the trees in the park and 2 is the park's area in hectares, $x$ must be the number of trees per hectare:
$$x = \frac{\text{trees in park}}{2} = \text{trees per hectare in park}$$
This is the average number of trees per hectare in the park.

That matches Choice A — The average number of trees per hectare in the park.
